mode_reason: Quick checks fired DISK WARNING (85% used, ≥80% threshold). Mode 2 selected per dispatcher rule. In execution it became clear the disk pressure is sandbox baseline (Ubuntu image + /var/log/journal/snapd accounting for ~7.5GB of /usr+/var) and not project-induced — agent clones in /tmp total 7.1MB. Pipeline itself is healthy and yesterday's two MAJORs both cleared. Mode 2 still right call because it surfaces the stale schedule_state and a fresh workspace-only review.

findings.description: priority-queue.json schedule_state has been stale since 2026-04-17 and is now further drifted by 2026-04-25's operator drain-mode change. The schedule_state block reports analyst=30m/curmudgeon=30m/decider=60m and an analyst_override_reason='canary-triggered-reactive-update' with note 'Churn-and-burn: dome author reactive update detected 2026-04-17. Canary soft-triggered. 5 change files pending analysis. Analyst/curmudgeon 30m, decider 1h.' Actual cron right now is analyst=60m, curmudgeon=240m (drain), decider=120m. Operator HNOTE-OPERATOR-DRAIN-MODE-001 (2026-04-25T09:03Z) has explicitly told the decider to stop re-flagging this as P1, and the latest decider report (09-32) complies — schedule_action_needed is no longer in the report. So the agent behavior is correct, but the on-disk schedule_state remains misleading for any future agent or human reading priority-queue.json.
findings.evidence: monitor/curmudgeon/priority-queue.json schedule_state.last_schedule_change=2026-04-17T16:43:23Z; analyst_current_interval_minutes=30; curmudgeon_current_interval_minutes=30; decider_current_interval_minutes=60. Live cron from list_scheduled_tasks: dome-analyst '50 * * * *' (60m), dome-curmudgeon '20 */4 * * *' (240m), dome-decider '15 */2 * * *' (120m). HNOTE-OPERATOR-DRAIN-MODE-001 monitor/decisions/human-notes.json 2026-04-25T09:03:50Z. Decider daily-report-2026-04-25T09-32.json has no schedule_action_needed field.

suggested_fix.replace: Decider should rewrite schedule_state on its next run to reflect actual cron (analyst 60m, curmudgeon 240m drain-mode, decider 120m), bump last_schedule_change to 2026-04-25T09:03Z, set last_schedule_change_by='operator', clear analyst_override_reason (the chg-20260417 backlog has been processed for days), and update note to reference HNOTE-OPERATOR-DRAIN-MODE-001 with cancellation criteria (queue 0 + open-issues <50 OR explicit operator HNOTE).
suggested_fix.rationale: schedule_state is decider-owned (writers: ['decider']). Tinker cannot self-apply. But leaving it stale risks confusing the next session's bootstrap and cluttering future agent reports.

findings.description: Workspace-only curmudgeon review monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/WIN-013.c4.json (20.7KB, mtime 2026-04-25T09:28Z, ~30 min before this tinker run) is not yet in git. Decider's daily report (09-32) shows WIN-013.c4 was popped at 09:28:12 and processed (qid 259, ISS-1538 'five years→two years' patch applied) — so the data has been consumed downstream, but the review file itself is at risk if the workspace recycles before workspace-sync's next 11:03Z run.
findings.evidence: ws md5 dc090ffd, GH HTTP 404. Integrity report-2026-04-25T08-18.json flagged this as MAJOR. workspace-sync nextRunAt 2026-04-25T11:03Z (~1h away).

suggested_fix.rationale: No-action: workspace-sync's 11:03Z run will sweep this up via 'sync_glob monitor/curmudgeon/reviews *.json'. If it doesn't appear in main by 11:30Z, escalate to manual `cp` from clean clone. Same pattern as WIN-067.c10.json yesterday — recovered automatically once workspace-sync ran.

findings.description: Disk usage 85% / 1.5GB free on the sandbox VM. This is a Mode 2 quick-check trigger but inspection shows it's the Ubuntu base image (/usr 6.1G, /var 1.7G of which /var/log/journal=588M, /var/lib/snapd=493M). Project-induced disk is /tmp/kev-repo 7.1MB and /tmp/dome-review-clean 4KB. No accumulated agent clones. The 80% WARNING threshold is calibrated for project-induced bloat; on this sandbox it's permanently above warning level even with no agent activity. Consider raising the threshold to 92% (i.e., trip only when project clones might actually run a tmpfs out of space) or routing the check through a project-relative path.
findings.evidence: df -h /: 8.1G used / 9.6G total. du -sh /usr=6.1G, /var=1.7G, /sessions=82M (workspace), /tmp=9.2M. /var/log/journal=588M (system journald). No agent-clone accumulation.

suggested_fix.find: if [ "$USE_PCT" -ge 80 ]; then echo "DISK WARNING: ≥80% used — one bad run from full"; fi
suggested_fix.replace: if [ "$USE_PCT" -ge 88 ]; then echo "DISK WARNING: ≥88% used — system+project disk; one bad run from full"; fi
suggested_fix.rationale: 85% is system baseline on this sandbox; 80% threshold trips Mode 2 every run regardless of project state, drowning out signal. 88% gives a 3pp buffer above baseline before raising warning.

findings.fix_reason_held: Threshold tuning is judgment, not mechanical. Operator may want the false-positive to surface at 80% so they notice if the sandbox image itself bloats. Will re-propose as a PROP if next 3 runs all trip Mode 2 on this same trigger.
